title: ferray-core: promotion of u128+i128 to f64 is lossy — document or find better fallback
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

In dtype/promotion.rs, u128+i128 promotes to f64. But f64 has a 52-bit mantissa, so values above 2^53 lose precision. NumPy doesn't have this problem because it doesn't have 128-bit integers. This is a known consequence of supporting u128/i128 (which is a ferray-specific extension beyond NumPy), but it should be documented. Consider: (1) adding a doc warning on result_type for these pairs, (2) returning an error instead of a lossy promotion, or (3) adding a DType::F128 if/when Rust stabilizes f128.
